sample.head:
# Cyber Data Breach Insurance for Dual PCI-DSS/HIPAA Exposure: Chubb vs. Coalition vs. Cowbell Cyber

**A Practical Evaluation for Austin-Based Small Businesses (15 Employees, $2–5M Revenue)**

---

## Why Your Risk Profile Is Unusual

Most small businesses carry either payment card data (PCI-DSS) or health records (HIPAA) — rarely both. You have a third layer as well: the Texas Data Privacy and Security Act (effective July 2024), which gives the Texas Attorney General independent enforcement authority and has been accompanied by increased enforcement activity. This creates three distinct regulatory rails that can trigger simultaneously from a single breach event.

Your HIPAA exposure deserves particular clarification: as an employer administering a group health plan or storing employee health benefit data, you are a HIPAA covered entity or business associate on the employer side — not only as a healthcare provider. Several policy forms treat HIPAA as a provider-only framework, which understates your actual exposure. Confirm with your broker that the policy explicitly covers employer-side PHI, not just patient records.

---

## Regulatory Fine Coverage and How Sublimits Work

### The Core Distinction
